name: Broadcom Inc.
employeeCount: ~33,000 worldwide as of Nov 2, 2025, ~57% in R&D (FY2025 10-K)
shortDescription: Designs custom AI/networking silicon and runs infrastructure software including VMware for hyperscalers and enterprises.
businessModel: Broadcom operates two segments: Semiconductor Solutions (58% of FY2025 revenue, $36.9B) spanning AI accelerators/networking, broadband, wireless, server storage, and industrial; and Infrastructure Software (42%, $27.0B) led by VMware plus mainframe, security, and Agile/DevOps tools. It monetizes silicon via custom ASIC/XPU design wins and merchant networking chips, and software via subscription/license conversion after shifting VMware to a subscription model.
marketPositioning: Broadcom is the leading merchant supplier of custom AI accelerators (XPUs) and AI networking silicon, positioned as the primary alternative to Nvidia's GPUs for hyperscalers building in-house chips. It competes with Marvell in custom ASICs/optical and networking, and historically with Qualcomm in wireless/connectivity; it leads Ethernet-based AI fabric switching (Tomahawk/Jericho) against Nvidia's InfiniBand/Spectrum.
growthDrivers: AI semiconductor revenue scaling rapidly - Q4 FY2025 AI revenue up 74% YoY, with Q1'26 guided to ~$8.2B (roughly doubling YoY)
Custom XPU/ASIC design wins with hyperscalers (Google, Meta, ByteDance) plus new OpenAI (10 GW) and Anthropic engagements expanding the customer roster
AI networking via Ethernet switching/optics (Tomahawk, Jericho) as the connectivity layer for large AI clusters
VMware monetization - conversion to subscription bundling (VCF) driving Infrastructure Software to $27.0B in FY2025
techDifferentiation: Broadcom pairs deep custom-silicon IP (SerDes, packaging, networking) with the engineering scale to co-design hyperscaler-specific XPUs that are smaller, cheaper, and more power-efficient than merchant GPUs. Its dominance in high-radix Ethernet switching gives it control of the AI cluster fabric, creating a hardware moat that is difficult and capital-intensive to replicate.

segmentRevenueMix.note: FY2025 $36,858M, ~58% of total revenue; includes AI accelerators, networking, broadband, wireless, storage
FY2025 $27,029M, ~42% of total revenue; Q4 FY2025 $6,943M up 26% YoY
~$19.9B in FY2025 (approx., sum of quarterly disclosures); Q4 up 74% YoY; Q1'26 guided ~$8.2B doubling YoY

leadership.name: Hock E. Tan
leadership.title: President and Chief Executive Officer
leadership.background: President, CEO and director since March 2006; previously CEO of Integrated Circuit Systems and held senior finance roles at Commodore, PepsiCo, and GM.

leadership.name: Kirsten M. Spears
leadership.title: Chief Financial Officer and Chief Accounting Officer
leadership.background: Sitting CFO and CAO; announced retirement effective June 12, 2026, after which she serves as a consultant. As of June 1, 2026 she remains CFO.

leadership.name: Amie Thuener
leadership.title: Chief Financial Officer (incoming, effective June 12, 2026)
leadership.background: Named to succeed Spears as CFO effective June 12, 2026; joined Broadcom May 4, 2026. Previously VP, Corporate Controller and Chief Accounting Officer at Alphabet since 2018.

sources.name: CNBC - Broadcom Q4 FY2025 earnings (AI chip sales to double)
CNBC - OpenAI partners with Broadcom on custom AI chips
Bloomberg - Meta, Broadcom deepen ties; Tan departs Meta board

dataConfidence: high - FY2025 segment revenue ($63.9B total; Semis $36.9B/58%; Software $27.0B/42%), Q4 figures, employee count (~33,000), HQ (Palo Alto), and CEO (Hock Tan, since 2006) confirmed from Broadcom official IR releases and FY2025 10-K. CFO transition (Spears retiring, Thuener succeeding effective June 12, 2026) confirmed from Broadcom 8-K/IR; Spears still sitting CFO as of June 1, 2026. XPU customers (Google, Meta, ByteDance, OpenAI, Anthropic) confirmed via CNBC/Bloomberg. Medium confidence on the ~$19.9B FY2025 AI-revenue total (summed from quarterly disclosures).
